creator: kbowling@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Michael from comment #14)

Michael, nobody, over multiple years, has any idea what you are after.  If there is a problem, it is buried in unnecessary technical opinions that are approximately meritless without providing some contribution to move the situation forward.

Ports is concerned with packaging external software and making it available on FreeBSD.  If you are upset that Nvidia dropped support for some card in 390.x, that is not a FreeBSD problem and there is nothing we can do.  Using 340.x is the correct action as a user.  But I cannot even tell if that is the issue, because there is so much extra opinion on what should and shouldn't be and what some other situation may or may not provide.

I took a look at https://salsa.debian.org/nvidia-team/nvidia-graphics-drivers/-/tree/390xx/debian/390.157-12/debian/module/debian/patches?ref_type=tags and nothing is jumping out to me as relevant to FreeBSD.  You posted an attachment about a compilation error but it seems like a local issue because we ship a building package for all supported releases.

You are going to need to do a lot more work to develop the issue to arrive at a valid contribution.
